assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable and payable are denominated in a foreign currency and give rise to exchange differences in the consolidated financial statements.
The question concerns the accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS Accounting Standards.

approaches.label: Hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context includes IFRS 9 hedge accounting requirements, including designation of hedged items and the specific treatment of intragroup foreign currency exposures in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Foreign currency translation accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context includes IAS 21 accounting for exchange differences on monetary items, including intragroup monetary balances in consolidated financial statements.
